column1: # Cross-Framework Mapping EU-DORA (2022/2554) <-> ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Annex A
# Source: EBA/EIOPA/ESMA Final Draft RTS/ITS on ICT Risk Management (2024)
# Scope: Focus on Art. 5-14 (ICT risk management

column9: rationale

DORA Art.5 ICT governance and risk management framework overlaps with ISO 27001 Clause 5.1 leadership and commitment. EBA-RTS binds the management body to the framework, matching top-management responsibility.
Policies must be documented and approved — DORA Art.5 demands written framework, A.5.1 delivers the documentation requirement.
DORA explicitly assigns accountability to the management body; A.5.4 management responsibilities is the ISO counterpart.
DORA Art.6 ICT risk management requires identification, assessment and treatment — full overlap with ISO 27001 6.1.2 risk assessment process.
Threat intelligence Art.6(2) maps to A.5.7 threat intelligence.
ICT systems identification and classification — DORA Art.7 vs A.8.1 user endpoint devices plus A.5.9 inventory.
Inventory of information and associated assets directly satisfies DORA Art.7 identification requirement.
DORA Art.8 protection and prevention — privileged access management maps to A.8.2.
Use of cryptography is part of Art.8 protection stack.
Secure authentication / MFA operationalises DORA Art.8(3) access controls.
Detection of anomalous activities — A.5.25 assessment and decision on information security events.
Monitoring activities cover Art.9 continuous detection.
Response and recovery — A.5.29 information security during disruption is the ISO-side baseline.
ICT readiness for business continuity, core of DORA Art.10.
Information backup underpins Art.10 recovery capability.
Redundancy of processing facilities supports DORA resilience targets.
ICT-related incident management process — direct map to A.5.24 incident response planning and preparation.
Response to incidents operationalises DORA Art.11 processes.
Learning from incidents satisfies DORA Art.11 continuous improvement obligation.
Classification of incidents — A.5.25 assessment and decision drives severity rating (DORA thresholds apply).

Reporting of major ICT incidents to authorities — A.6.8 information security event reporting provides the operational channel. Note: DORA adds 4h initial / 72h intermediate / 1-month final deadlines not in ISO.
Evidence collection supports authority reporting workflow.
ICT-related security awareness and training — direct A.6.3 map.
Digital operational resilience testing programme — A.8.29 security testing in development and acceptance provides the core ISO practice (DORA expands to TLPT for significant entities).
Vulnerability assessments and scans — part of A.8.29.
TLPT Threat-Led Penetration Testing — A.8.29 covers pen-test discipline; DORA prescribes red-team scope beyond ISO.
Third-party ICT risk management principles — direct overlap with A.5.19 information security in supplier relationships.
Addressing information security within supplier agreements — core DORA Art.28 contractual requirement.
ICT supply chain — directly mapped.
Monitoring, review and change management of supplier services.
Cloud services as sub-category of third-party ICT services.
Register of information on ICT third-party providers — ISO lifecycle covers identification but not the DORA-specific ROI fields (LEI, substitutability, subcontracting chain).
Key contractual provisions for ICT services — direct bridge to A.5.20 content requirements.
Subcontracting of ICT services supporting critical/important functions — monitoring the subcontracting chain aligns with A.5.22.
Information sharing arrangements on cyber threat intelligence — A.5.7 coverage.
